  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "The development and deployment of ML systems is typically rushed and means-to-an-end.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the process is typically rushed and", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "Lack of diligence can lead to technical debt, scope creep, misaligned objectives, model misuse and failures, and expensive consequences.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "The lack of diligence can lead", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "ML models are typically trained and tested on only a handful of curated datasets, without safeguards for future scenarios.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "models are typically trained and tested on only", "location": "1. Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "Code quality in ML development is typically subpar and poorly documented.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Code quality is typically subpar", "location": "1. Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "The NASA technology readiness process is overkill for ML/software workflows.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "readiness process is overkill.", "location": "1. Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "The TRL of a system is the lowest level of its constituent parts.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "TRL of the systems is the lowest level of its constituent parts.", "location": "2. TRL4ML"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "Most ML projects do not start at level 0; very few companies engage in low-level theoretical research.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "do not start at level 0", "location": "2.1. Key components in the process"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "No technology can skip levels after the TRL4ML process has been initiated.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "levels after the TRL4ML process has been initiated.", "location": "2.1. Key components in the process"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "A team using an off-the-shelf object recognition model would start that technology at level 4.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "an off-the-shelf object recognition model would start", "location": "2.1. Key components in the process"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "The behavior of ML systems is learned from data, not specified directly in code.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the behavior of ML systems is learned from", "location": "3. Discussion"},
    {"id": "cl11", "claim_text": "There is nearly always a non-trivial gap in transferring a model from a simulation testbed to the real world.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "is nearly always a non-trivial gap in transferring a model", "location": "2.1.
